songs.title: Welcome to New York
songs.lyrics:
Walkin' through a crowd, the village is aglow
Kaleidoscope of loud heartbeats under coats
Everybody here wanted somethin' more
Searchin' for a sound we hadn't heard before
And it said

Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York
Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York
It's a new soundtrack, I could dance to this beat, beat forevermore
The lights are so bright, but they never blind me, me
Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York

When we first dropped our bags on apartment floors
Took our broken hearts, put them in a drawer
Everybody here was someone else before
And you can want who you want
Boys and boys and girls and girls

Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York
Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York
It's a new soundtrack, I could dance to this beat, beat forevermore
The lights are so bright, but they never blind me, me
Welcome to New York (New York), it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York

Like any great love, it keeps you guessing
Like any real love, it's ever-changing
Like any true love, it drives you crazy
But you know you wouldn't change anything, anything, anything

Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York
Welcome to New York, it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York
It's a new soundtrack, I could dance to this beat
The lights are so bright, but they never blind me
Welcome to New York (New soundtrack), it's been waitin' for you
Welcome to New York, the lights are so bright, but they never blind me
Welcome to New York, so bright, they never blind me, yeah
Welcome to New York, welcome to New York

vocab.definition: A colorful, constantly changing pattern of shapes and colors, often created by reflecting light through a rotating tube with loose, colored materials inside. In a broader sense, it can refer to a multicolored, dynamic, and constantly changing situation or environment.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Kaleidoscope of loud heartbeats
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'kaleidoscope' to describe the vibrant and dynamic atmosphere of New York, emphasizing its diversity and constant change. This word helps to set the tone for the rest of the song, which explores themes of self-discovery and exploration in the city.

vocab.definition: Giving off a warm, soft, and steady light, often used to describe something that is radiant or glowing with health and vitality.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the village is aglow
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'aglow' to describe the warm and inviting atmosphere of the village, setting the tone for a sense of excitement and possibility. This word helps to create a vivid image of the city at night, full of energy and life.

vocab.definition: Always changing or altering, often in unpredictable or unexpected ways. This phrase can be used to describe something that is dynamic, flexible, and adaptable.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Like any real love, it's ever-changing
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'ever-changing' to describe the nature of love and relationships in New York, highlighting the city's fast-paced and unpredictable environment. This word helps to emphasize the idea that love and relationships are complex and multifaceted, and that they can be both exciting and challenging.

vocab.definition: For all future time, or from now on. This word is often used to emphasize a sense of permanence or continuity.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I could dance to this beat, beat forevermore
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'forevermore' to express her enthusiasm and dedication to the city and its energy, suggesting that she could happily stay in this state of excitement and joy indefinitely. This word helps to create a sense of timelessness and freedom, emphasizing the idea that anything is possible in New York.

songs.title: Blank Space
songs.lyrics:
Nice to meet you, where you been?
I could show you incredible things
Magic, madness, heaven, sin
Saw you there and I thought
"Oh, my God, look at that face
You look like my next mistake
Love's a game, wanna play?" Ayy
New money, suit and tie
I can read you like a magazine
Ain't it funny? Rumors fly
And I know you heard about me
So, hey, let's be friends
I'm dying to see how this one ends
Grab your passport and my hand
I can make the bad guys good for a weekend

So it's gonna be forever
Or it's gonna go down in flames?

They'll tell you I'm insane
'Cause you know I love the players

It'll leave you breathless, mm

And I'll write your name

Cherry lips, crystal skies
I could show you incredible things
Stolen kisses, pretty lies
You're the king, baby, I'm your queen

Wait, the worst is yet to come, oh, no

I can make all the tables turn
Rose garden filled with thorns
Keep you second guessing, like
"Oh, my God, who is she?"
I get drunk on jealousy
But you'll come back each time you leave
'Cause, darling, I'm a nightmare dressed like a daydream

So it's gonna be forever
Or it's gonna go down in flames?
You can tell me when it's over, mm
If the high was worth the pain
Got a long list of ex-lovers
They'll tell you I'm insane
'Cause you know I love the players
And you love the game
'Cause we're young and we're reckless (Oh)
We'll take this way too far
It'll leave you breathless (Oh-oh), mm
Or with a nasty scar
Got a long list of ex-lovers
They'll tell you I'm insane (Insane)
But I've got a blank space, baby
And I'll write your name

Boys only want love if it's torture
Don't say I didn't, say I didn't warn ya
Boys only want love if it's torture
Don't say I didn't, say I didn't warn ya

So it's gonna be forever
Or it's gonna go down in flames?
You can tell me when it's over (Over), mm
If the high was worth the pain
Got a long list of ex-lovers
They'll tell you I'm insane (I'm insane)
'Cause you know I love the players

'Cause we're young and we're reckless (Yeah)

Or with a nasty scar (With a nasty scar)

They'll tell you I'm insane

And I'll write your name

vocab.definition: Having or showing no care for the consequences of an action; impulsive and irresponsible.
vocab.lyric_snippet: we're young and we're reckless
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the all-consuming and potentially destructive nature of her relationship, implying a willingness to take risks and disregard potential harm.

vocab.definition: Unpleasant or disagreeable, often in a way that is shocking or disturbing.
vocab.lyric_snippet: or with a nasty scar
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to hint at the potential negative consequences of her relationship, suggesting that it could leave emotional scars.

vocab.definition: Severe physical or mental suffering, often inflicted intentionally.
vocab.lyric_snippet: boys only want love if it's torture
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to suggest that some people are drawn to relationships that are painful or all-consuming, rather than healthy and fulfilling.

vocab.definition: A feeling of resentment or bitterness towards someone because of their success or perceived advantage.
vocab.lyric_snippet: i get drunk on jealousy
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the intense emotions that can arise in a relationship, implying a sense of possessiveness or insecurity.

vocab.definition: A frightening or unpleasant dream, or a situation that is very unpleasant or difficult to deal with.
vocab.lyric_snippet: darling, i'm a nightmare dressed like a daydream
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to suggest that she may appear attractive or appealing on the surface, but has a darker or more troubled side.

vocab.definition: A pleasant and often unrealistic fantasy or reverie, typically experienced while awake.
vocab.lyric_snippet: darling, i'm a nightmare dressed like a daydream
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to contrast with the idea of a nightmare, implying that she may appear pleasant or appealing, but has a darker reality.

vocab.definition: A place or state of supreme happiness or bliss. It can also refer to a place of beauty or delight.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Could end in burning flames or paradise
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'paradise' to describe the potential positive outcome of her relationship, contrasting it with the negative outcome of 'burning flames'.

vocab.definition: Made smooth and flat, typically using a substance such as hair gel or water. It can also refer to something that is cleverly or deceitfully done.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You got that long hair, slicked back, white T-shirt
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'slicked' to describe her partner's hairstyle, emphasizing his stylish and attractive appearance.

vocab.definition: Falling or coming down violently, often with a loud noise. It can also refer to a situation that is failing or collapsing.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And when we go crashing down, we come back every time
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'crashing' to describe the ups and downs of her relationship, emphasizing the cyclical nature of their romance.

vocab.definition: A type of instant camera that produces a physical photograph after taking a picture. The term can also refer to the photograph itself.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You took a Polaroid of us
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a moment in her relationship where a photo was taken, capturing a memory. The use of 'Polaroid' specifically adds a nostalgic and retro feel to the narrative.

vocab.definition: Frightening or intimidating entities, often used figuratively to describe problems or challenges.
vocab.lyric_snippet: But the monsters turned out to be just trees
vocab.context: In this context, Taylor uses 'monsters' to represent the obstacles and fears in her relationship, which ultimately turned out to be less intimidating than they seemed.

vocab.definition: Sutures used to close a wound, often requiring medical attention.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Twenty stitches in the hospital room
vocab.context: Taylor mentions 'stitches' to recall a specific, possibly traumatic event in her relationship, emphasizing the depth of their experiences together.

vocab.definition: The inner surface of the hand that extends from the wrist to the base of the fingers. Figuratively, it can also mean to have someone or something under one's control.
vocab.lyric_snippet: had me in the palm of your hand
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'palm' to convey how she was completely under her partner's control and influence. The phrase suggests a sense of vulnerability and surrender.

vocab.definition: To a position away from the main area or activity. It can also mean to ignore or dismiss something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the love they pushed aside
vocab.context: In this context, 'aside' implies that the person has ignored or dismissed the love they once had, only to want it back later.

vocab.definition: For all time, or for an extremely long time. It can also be used to emphasize the permanence of a situation or feeling.
vocab.lyric_snippet: people like me are gone forever
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'forever' to stress that once she's gone, she's gone for good, and the relationship cannot be rekindled.

vocab.definition: Hella is an informal adjective used to emphasize a quality, similar to 'very' or 'extremely'. It is often used in informal settings or in regional dialects.
vocab.lyric_snippet: fella over there with the hella good hair
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'hella' to describe someone's hair as exceptionally good, showcasing her casual and playful tone in the song.

vocab.definition: In this context, 'sick' is a slang term meaning exceptional or impressive, often used to describe something that is considered cool or of high quality.
vocab.lyric_snippet: gettin' down to this sick beat
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'sick' to compliment the music, highlighting her carefree and upbeat attitude in the song.

vocab.definition: A person who pretends to have qualities or characteristics that they do not actually possess, often to deceive or manipulate others.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the fakers gonna fake, fake, fake, fake, fake
vocab.context: Taylor mentions 'fakers' as a group of people who are insincere, contrasting them with her own genuine attitude and carefree nature.

vocab.definition: Groovin' refers to the act of moving or dancing to music, often in a smooth or rhythmic way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Can't stop, won't stop groovin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'groovin'' to describe her ability to keep dancing and enjoying herself, despite what others might say or think.

vocab.definition: Not straight or direct; having an irregular or uneven shape. In a broader sense, it can also imply a deviation from what is considered normal or proper.
vocab.lyric_snippet: We're a crooked love in a straight line down
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'crooked' to describe the unconventional nature of her relationship, highlighting the complexities and irregularities that make it unique.

vocab.definition: Extremely angry or furious. However, in this context, 'mad' is used to emphasize the intensity of the love rather than anger.
vocab.lyric_snippet: This mad, mad love makes you come runnin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'mad' to convey the all-consuming and overwhelming nature of her love, which drives her and her partner to return to each other despite challenges.

vocab.definition: Emotional triggers or sensitive topics that can provoke a reaction. The phrase 'push someone's buttons' means to intentionally annoy or provoke them.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You always knew how to push my buttons
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'buttons' to express how her partner knows exactly what to say or do to elicit an emotional response from her, indicating a deep understanding of her emotions.

vocab.definition: Covered with or affected by rust, which is a reddish-brown substance that forms on metal when it oxidizes. It can also be used figuratively to describe something that has become old, worn out, or decayed.
vocab.lyric_snippet: What was shiny? Now it's all rusted
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'rusted' to describe how a once beautiful relationship has deteriorated and become worn out. The word is used to convey a sense of decay and deterioration.

vocab.definition: Marks left on the skin or on a surface after a wound has healed. It can also be used figuratively to describe emotional or psychological damage.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Still got scars on my back from your knife
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'scars' to describe the lasting emotional pain caused by a past hurt. The word is used to convey a sense of permanent damage.

vocab.definition: The spirit of a dead person, or a person who is felt to be present but is not seen. It can also be used figuratively to describe a lingering feeling or memory.
vocab.lyric_snippet: If you live like that, you live with ghosts
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'ghosts' to describe the lingering emotional pain and guilt that can haunt someone who has hurt others. The word is used to convey a sense of unresolved issues.

vocab.definition: A small piece of sticky material used to cover and protect a small wound or cut.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Band-Aids don't fix bullet holes
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'band-aids' to describe superficial or inadequate attempts to fix deep emotional wounds. The word is used to convey a sense of inadequacy.

vocab.definition: Having a pleasing and attractive appearance, especially in a strong and masculine way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: He's so tall and handsome as hell
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the physical appearance of the person she's interested in, emphasizing his attractive qualities.

vocab.definition: Well-known or recognized because of being seen or experienced many times before.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And his voice is a familiar sound
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to convey a sense of comfort and recognition in the relationship, highlighting the familiarity of the person's voice.

vocab.definition: The ability to understand or see something clearly only after it has happened.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You'll see me in hindsight
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express the idea that the person will only truly appreciate or understand the relationship after it has ended.

vocab.definition: Twisted or caught together in a complicated way, often making it difficult to separate or understand.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Tangled up with you all night
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the complex and intimate nature of the relationship, emphasizing the emotional connection between the two people.

vocab.definition: Having a healthy and pink color, often used to describe someone's cheeks.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Red lips and rosy cheeks
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to create a vivid image of the person she's describing, emphasizing their healthy and attractive appearance.

vocab.definition: completely uncontrolled or unrestrained; most extreme or intense
vocab.lyric_snippet: in silent screams and wildest dreams
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the intensity of her dreams, emphasizing the depth of her emotions. The phrase 'wildest dreams' suggests a strong, all-consuming passion.

vocab.definition: shone or burned unsteadily, with a wavering light; to have existed or been noticeable for only a short time
vocab.lyric_snippet: lantern, burning flickered in my mind
vocab.context: The word 'flickered' is used to convey the idea that the memory of the person is still present in Taylor's mind, but it's not a constant or stable presence. The image of a flickering lantern suggests a fragile, fleeting light.

vocab.definition: lasting or remaining unchanged forever; intended to last or remain unchanged for a long time
vocab.lyric_snippet: this love left a permanent mark
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'permanent' to describe the lasting impact of the love on her life. The phrase 'permanent mark' suggests a deep, indelible impression that cannot be erased.

vocab.definition: shining with a soft, steady, and warm light; radiant with health, happiness, or enthusiasm
vocab.lyric_snippet: this love is glowing in the dark
vocab.context: The word 'glowing' is used to describe the love as a source of light and warmth, even in difficult or dark times. The image of something glowing in the dark suggests a powerful, enduring presence.

vocab.definition: going down or downwards, especially into a liquid or a state of decline or failure
vocab.lyric_snippet: been losing grip on sinking ships
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'sinking' to describe a situation that is failing or deteriorating. The metaphor of 'sinking ships' suggests a sense of desperation and loss of control.

vocab.definition: Birds of prey that feed on carrion, often seen as a symbol of danger or threat. In a broader sense, people who take advantage of others' misfortunes.
vocab.lyric_snippet: See the vultures circling, dark clouds
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'vultures' to describe the people who are waiting for her relationship to fail, circling and waiting to pounce on any sign of weakness. This highlights the external pressures and threats to her relationship.

vocab.definition: Easily broken or damaged, often used to describe something delicate or vulnerable.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Love's a fragile little flame
vocab.context: Taylor describes love as a 'fragile little flame' to convey its vulnerability and the ease with which it can be extinguished. This emphasizes the need for care and protection in relationships.

vocab.definition: Enclosures used to confine or restrict something, often used metaphorically to describe feelings of confinement or limitation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They got the cages, they got the boxes and guns
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'cages' to describe the ways in which society or external forces can restrict or trap individuals, particularly in the context of her relationship. This highlights the need for freedom and escape.

vocab.definition: Not tightly closed or fastened, often used to describe something that is careless or indiscreet.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Loose lips sink ships all the damn time
vocab.context: Taylor references the phrase 'loose lips sink ships' to caution against careless talk or gossip, which can have negative consequences. This emphasizes the importance of discretion and secrecy in her relationship.

vocab.definition: Completely protected or invulnerable, often used to describe something that is resistant to attack or criticism.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They take their shots, but we're bulletproof
vocab.context: Taylor describes her relationship as 'bulletproof' to convey its strength and resilience in the face of external challenges or criticisms. This highlights the couple's ability to withstand adversity.

vocab.definition: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall, leading to water shortage. In a broader sense, it can also refer to a period of scarcity or lack.
vocab.lyric_snippet: The drought was the very worst
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'drought' to describe a period of emotional dryness and scarcity in her relationship, where the 'flowers' of their love have withered and died.

vocab.definition: A strong feeling of wanting or needing something, especially a drink. It can also be used figuratively to describe a strong desire or craving.
vocab.lyric_snippet: died of thirst
vocab.context: The 'flowers' in the relationship have 'died of thirst', symbolizing the emotional unfulfillment and longing that has led to the end of the relationship.

vocab.definition: Not intoxicated or drunk. In a broader sense, it can also mean being in a state of calmness and clarity, free from emotional turmoil.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Ten months sober
vocab.context: Taylor mentions being 'ten months sober', indicating a period of emotional recovery and clarity after the end of the relationship.

vocab.definition: Not explicitly mentioned, but 'drought' implies a state of scarcity.
vocab.lyric_snippet: The drought was the very worst
